title: Device API & wiring
lead: This page documents how devices integrate with Flovers. Devices send sensor readings to the ingest endpoint and can read the latest device feed from the feed endpoint, for example to trigger actions such as launching a water pump. You’ll also find the payload format and the same Arduino firmware structure that is generated for device setup.

endpoints.title: API endpoints
endpoints.note: Devices send telemetry to the ingest endpoint and can read the latest device feed from the feed endpoint.

ingest.title: POST ingest documentation
ingest.desc: Send one reading sample as JSON. The backend authenticates the device using device_key and secret, then stores the provided metrics under that device.

feed.title: GET feed documentation
feed.desc: Read the latest device feed from the backend. Devices can use this endpoint to receive current state or actions, for example launching a water pump.

arduino.title: Arduino (ESP32/ESP8266) generated firmware
arduino.desc: The generated firmware connects to Wi-Fi, synchronizes time with NTP, reads enabled sensors, and posts JSON to the ingest endpoint on a fixed interval. The same device can also read the feed endpoint to retrieve the latest actionable state from the backend.
arduino.note: Use the generated firmware as provided for the device. Before uploading, set Wi-Fi credentials and verify sensor wiring, pin assignments, and soil calibration values.

sensors.moisture: Capacitive soil moisture sensor — analog read with wet/dry calibration
sensors.bme280: BME280 — temperature & humidity over I2C (pressure optional)

wiring.title: Wiring diagram
wiring.desc: Add a Fritzing wiring image here later. This placeholder keeps layout stable until the diagram is ready.
wiring.placeholderSub: Replace this box with an image when you export the wiring diagram.
